summary: The primary reason hypotonic solutions should NOT be used in burns is because they can lead to significant risks including tissue swelling without providing any clear benefit compared to more appropriate alternatives like lactated ringers which better mimic intravascular volume needs while minimizing potential harm from excess hydration. This aligns well with standard medical practice guidelines emphasizing safe effective treatments tailored specifically towards managing thermal injuries optimally through careful selection based upon
